metadata.title:
A DUTCH IMMIGRANT FAMILY ON THE HUNTLEY PROJECT
- NOTE THE CHIf.DRE MS WOODEN SHOES.

pages.raw_text:
are trying to hold down both
claims. He has to live here and
she 60 miles away. But they visit
back and forth. and they will have
two farms instead of one.


THAT prosperous place over
there is the home of old Sol Smith.
He came from Missouri, but had so
little idea Of irrigation farming that
he supposed the government wat
cred his crops for him. He came
to the office of the supervisor and
said he supposed that all he had to
do was to plant, and let the gov
eminent do the rest He is doing
fine.


This place-you see 1t is one Of
the most prosperous We have seen
belongs to- a Norwegian from st.
Paul He came last year. but had
hard luck. So he wrote back to the
girl in. st. Paul to whom he was
engaged, saying things had all gone
badly and they would have to post
pone their wedding another year.
To his surprise he received tele-
gram saying: If you ever need
me IL is now. Meet me at the 9:15
train She is doing half the work,
and they are apparently as happy
as any two people in the United
States.


Here is the school teachers
farm. He worbks II after hours. You
see he has put in a little of every
thing you can think of.
